description: Replaces all read groups in the input file with a new read group and assigns all reads to this read group in the output
author: Picard Tools
plan:
## Picard.AddOrReplaceReadGroups GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
Picard.AddOrReplaceReadGroups is a GenePattern module that Replaces all read groups in the input file with a new read group and assigns all reads to this read group in the output

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 9 parameters:
- Required: input.file, read.group.id, read.group.library, read.group.platform, read.group.platform.unit, read.group.sample.name, output.prefix
- Optional: read.group.sequencing.center.name, read.group.description

### 3. Command Line
```
<java> <java_flags> -cp <libdir>AddOrReplaceReadGroups.jar<path.separator><libdir>commons-cli-1.2.jar<path.separator><libdir>commons-lang-2.5.jar<path.separator><libdir>gp-AddOrReplaceReadGroups.jar org.genepattern.sam.AddOrReplaceReadGroupsWrapper -inputFileName <input.file> -groupId <read.group.id> -lib <read.group.library> -platform <read.group.platform> -pUnit <read.group.platform.unit> -sample <read.group.sample.name> -center <read.group.sequencing.center.name> -desc <read.group.description> -outputFileName <output.prefix>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: 

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: read.group.library
parameters.description: DNA preparation library. Note that this field is required by the module Picard.MarkDuplicates. It is used to determine which read groups may contain molecular duplicates, in the case where the same DNA library was sequenced on multiple lanes.

parameters.name: read.group.platform.unit
parameters.description: Read Group platform unit (e.g., run barcode).

parameters.name: output.prefix
parameters.description: A label used to name the output file
